perturbation.original_text: Except to the extent permitted by Section 3 below, Independent Contractor will not disclose the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Except to the extent permitted by Section 3 below, Independent Contractor will not disclose the Data, in whole or in part, to any other party.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Data' in this section creates ambiguity. 'Data' is not explicitly defined, while 'Confidential Information' is supposed to be. This change can be interpretd as refering to any data and not just confidential data, leading to a different enforcement of the clause, and creating an in-text contradiction with the overall scope of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall be deemed not to include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than (a) as a result of a disclosure by Independent Contractor or any other person who directly or indirectly receives such information from the Independent Contractor or (b) in violation of a confidentiality obligation to the Company known to Independent Contractor or (ii) is or becomes available to Independent Contractor on a non-confidential basis from a source which is entitled to disclose it to Independent Contractor or (iii) is independently developed by Independent Contractor without benefit of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is a defined term elsewhere in the contract, specifying what is included and excluded. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Data,' which is not defined. This creates a contradiction because the clause now refers to an ambiguous term, potentially covering a broader range of information than intended and conflicting with the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted text. The contradicted text defines what is not considered Confidential Information, and the change introduces a new, undefined term. It is an instance of misaligned terminology leading to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, Independent Contractor shall use at least the same standard of care it uses to protect its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.changed_text: In fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, Independent Contractor shall use industry best practices to protect sensitive company information, but under no circumstances employ less than commercially reasonable security measures.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the standard of care from protecting 'its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care' to using 'industry best practices to protect sensitive company information, but under no circumstances employ less than commercially reasonable security measures' introduces ambiguity. The new wording doesn't reference the contractor's own practices, which could be lower. This change introduces vagueness in determining the standard of care, leading to a different enforcement of the clause and an in-text contradiction within the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Independent Contractor shall use the Proprietary Data solely for the purpose of performing the Services specified in the applicable SOW and not for any other purpose. Except to the extent permitted by Section 3 below, Independent Contractor will not disclose the Data, in whole or in part, to any other party. In fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, Independent Contractor shall use industry best practices to protect sensitive company information, but under no circumstances employ less than commercially reasonable security measures. 2. The term "Confidential Information" shall be deemed not to include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than (a) as a result of a disclosure by Independent Contractor or any other person who directly or indirectly receives such information from the Independent Contractor or (b) in violation of a confidentiality obligation to the Company known to Independent Contractor or (ii) is or becomes available to Independent Contractor on a non-confidential basis from a source which is entitled to disclose it to Independent Contractor or (iii) is independently developed by Independent Contractor without benefit of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from using 'the same standard of care it uses to protect its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care' to 'industry best practices to protect sensitive company information, but under no circumstances employ less than commercially reasonable security measures' introduces an ambiguity regarding the standard of care. While the Contradicted Text references the new changed text as a required standard of care, the modified language creates uncertainty around the original clause by not referencing the contractor's own practices. The changed text has deviated from the original standard, creating an in-text contradiction within the agreement. This aligns with Misaligned Terminology, as it uses the terms 'reasonable standard of care' and 'industry best practices' differently without proper definition, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall be deemed not to include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than (a) as a result of a disclosure by Independent Contractor or any other person who directly or indirectly receives such information from the Independent Contractor or (b) in violation of a confidentiality obligation to the Company known to Independent Contractor or (ii) is or becomes available to Independent Contractor on a non-confidential basis from a source which is entitled to disclose it to Independent Contractor or (iii) is independently developed by Independent Contractor without benefit of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall be deemed to include all information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than (a) as a result of a disclosure by Independent Contractor or any other person who directly or indirectly receives such information from the Independent Contractor or (b) in violation of a confidentiality obligation to the Company known to Independent Contractor or (ii) is or becomes available to Independent Contractor on a non-confidential basis from a source which is entitled to disclose it to Independent Contractor or (iii) is independently developed by Independent Contractor without benefit of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Confidential Information" is changed to include information that is publicly available. This directly contradicts the purpose of a non-disclosure agreement, which is to protect information that is *not* publicly available. The original definition specifically *excluded* publicly available information. Now it *includes* it, which is internally inconsistent with the entire agreement's purpose. Changing the definition of what the parties initially agreed to protect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company. Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B. 5. Independent Contractor acknowledges and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor and that the Company shall be entitled to specific performance, including without limitation injunctive relief, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Exhibit B but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Independent Contractor agrees to reimburse the Company for costs and expenses (including without limitation attorneys' fees) incurred by the Company in connection with the enforcement of this Exhibit B. 6. If any provision of this Exhibit B is not enforceable in whole or in part, the remaining provisions of this Exhibit B shall not be affected thereby. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege hereunder. 4
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Confidential Information" to *include* publicly available information, where the original definition specifically *excluded* such information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines obligations for handling "Confidential Information," such as returning or destroying it upon termination. If publicly available information is now considered confidential, the obligation to protect such information becomes nonsensical and unenforceable, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: All Private Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information' in the first sentence of this section creates ambiguity. While the second sentence retains 'Confidential Information', there's now a question of whether the obligation to return applies to all 'Confidential Information' or only the 'Private Information' initially mentioned. This makes the enforceability of returning all confidential material uncertain, leading to an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Private Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company. Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B. 5. Independent Contractor acknowledges and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor and that the Company shall be entitled to specific performance, including without limitation injunctive relief, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Exhibit B but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Independent Contractor agrees to reimburse the Company for costs and expenses (including without limitation attorneys' fees) incurred by the Company in connection with the enforcement of this Exhibit B. 6. If any provision of this Exhibit B is not enforceable in whole or in part, the remaining provisions of this Exhibit B shall not be affected thereby. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege hereunder. 4
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text were identical, but the change of 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information' in the first sentence while retaining 'Confidential Information' in subsequent sentences creates a contradiction. It introduces ambiguity regarding which information, private or confidential, the obligation to return and destroy applies to. This ambiguity weakens the enforceability of the confidential information clause and creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear if Independent Contractor is responsible for returning only 'Private Information' or all 'Confidential Information' as outlined in the contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
